title: clinopodium repens
intro: Clinopodium repens, also known as creeping calamint or wild basil, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing digestive problems like indigestion and nausea. Clinopodium repens can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
